learning_outcomes.description: Articulate the foundational knowledge in the area of entrepreneurship.
Develop, evaluate and communicate actionable business opportunities as part of a startup team.
Create viable and innovative Business Models.
Critically analyse knowledge creation in the startup process.
Practice real-time entrepreneurial leadership to craft ideas and test impacts.

assessments.due_date:
Pitch 20/08/2026

Video 8/10/2026 2:00 pm

Each presentation earns up to 20 marks against the A1 rubric
assessments.task_description:
The presentation arc requires a facilitated pitch delivered in real-time to your peers (deliver in week 4), and a video articulating your venture growth strategy (upload in week 10) . Each presentation format evidences your critical thinking for your team venture and attract up to 20 marks each, against the A1 rubric. The rubric also assesses inclusive team contribution under the Respectful Leaders graduate attribute

Within each team, the impact of each team member will be appraised using the Buddycheck platform integrated to Blackboard, generating an individual peer assessment factor (PAF). The assigned team score for each presentation x your individual PAF will yield your individual mark for each of the pitch & video.

This assessment will evaluate students’ Influential Communicator skills for UQ Business School Assurance of Learning (AoL) program, which maintains accreditation with AACSB and collects student grades in relevant criteria for quality measurement of student learning outcomes across the School. This program ensures that students attain the UQ Graduate Attributes, through alignment with UQ Business School program learning outcomes, and the School continuously improves curriculum.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Each presentation format is uploaded via Blackboard (to allow marking allocations & peer appraisal via Buddycheck)
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Case topic 1 3/09/2026 - 10/09/2026

Case topic 2 17/09/2026 - 24/09/2026

Case topic 3 8/10/2026 - 15/10/2026

Case topic 4 22/10/2026 - 29/10/2026

Each student generates ONE resource and moderates THREE resources of other students, for each case topic, within each available date range. Each case topic attracts up to 5 marks.
assessments.task_description: For each of the 4 case topics, generate a resource using the aligned entrepreneurial theory. Your peers will moderate the resource you produce, and you will moderate three of theirs (up to 5 marks per case topic). These case topics serve as a safe learning environment to test your thinking and gain moderated feedback from the class, using the A2 rubric. The moderation work also develops real-time entrepreneurial leadership — substantive, calibrated engagement with the reasoning of your peers, rather than templated politeness. Each of the 4 case topics will be released in Blackboard with resource creation and moderation completed in RiPPLE.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Use the RiPPLE link provided in Blackboard>Assessment for each case topic
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Details for a live case will be released prior to the designated exam time, allowing you to research the case with the resources provided and to prepare your notes for the exam, to demonstrate your analytical thinking. One double-sided page of handwritten or types notes is permitted to be taken into the exam and must be submitted along with your completed exam paper.

The exam will evaluate students’ Courageous Thinkers skills for UQ Business School Assurance of Learning (AoL) program, which maintains accreditation with AACSB and collects student grades in relevant criteria for quality measurement of student learning outcomes across the School. This program ensures that students attain the UQ Graduate Attributes, through alignment with UQ Business School program learning outcomes, and the School continuously improves curriculum.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit completed answers to the invigilator at the end of the exam session, including your written/typed notes, rough working paper & question sheet with any markings.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: The exam paper with your notes must be handed to the invigilator at the close of the exam session.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
S1 | New Ventures

Introduce entrepreneurship, a history of entrepreneurial thought and its place in today's economy. Opportunity discovery & creation. Projects to develop. Assessment timelines. Student team formation.
- Difference between small firms, entrepreneurial ventures and larger
corporations
- Entrepreneurial teams

Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: S1 | New Ventures Introduce entrepreneurship, a history of entrepreneurial thought and its place in today's economy. Opportunity discovery & creation. Projects to develop. Assessment timelines. Student team formation. - Difference between small firms, entrepreneurial ventures and larger corporations - Entrepreneurial teams

learning_activities.topic:
S2 | Lean business models

Using lean startup methods to shape business models and expose underlying growth drivers of value. Building a business model canvas informed using lean methods


Learning outcomes: L01, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: S2 | Lean business models Using lean startup methods to shape business models and expose underlying growth drivers of value. Building a business model canvas informed using lean methods

learning_activities.topic:
S3 | Discovery-driven Planning

Developing assumptions to bring new products & services to market. Crafting an idea canvas. Exploring a DDP case.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: S3 | Discovery-driven Planning Developing assumptions to bring new products & services to market. Crafting an idea canvas. Exploring a DDP case.

learning_activities.topic:
S4 | Present venture pitch

Deliver class presentation in your teams, showing how business model enables growth


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S4 | Present venture pitch Deliver class presentation in your teams, showing how business model enables growth

learning_activities.topic:
S5 | Entrepreneurial AI

Generative AI tools positioned as a new category of means within an effectual decision logic. We use AI to devise and evaluate new venture pathways through affordable-loss experiments and stakeholder rehearsals, building the critical judgement to recognise where AI overstates what it can actually deliver.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S5 | Entrepreneurial AI Generative AI tools positioned as a new category of means within an effectual decision logic. We use AI to devise and evaluate new venture pathways through affordable-loss experiments and stakeholder rehearsals, building the critical judgement to recognise where AI overstates what it can actually deliver.

learning_activities.topic:
S6 | Entrepreneurial Strategy

Entrepreneurial strategy frameworks: forming opportunity to gain competitive advantage & growth. Framing observed alignments to the four value-creation dynamics across customer-technology-capabilities-competition elements.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: S6 | Entrepreneurial Strategy Entrepreneurial strategy frameworks: forming opportunity to gain competitive advantage & growth. Framing observed alignments to the four value-creation dynamics across customer-technology-capabilities-competition elements.

learning_activities.topic:
S7 | Business Building

Applying a discovery-driven growth orientation to scale with technology and business models. In-session work focuses on the team venture's build plan.
learning_activities.topic_description: S7 | Business Building Applying a discovery-driven growth orientation to scale with technology and business models. In-session work focuses on the team venture's build plan.

learning_activities.topic:
S8 | Entrepreneurial Leadership

Drivers for design-led growth strategies and how to organise for emergent leadership paths in entrepreneurial ventures. Two organisational philosophies contrasted. In-class strategic simulation provides lived experience of leadership choices under uncertainty, with facilitator-scaffolded debrief on the choices observed and their consequences across the simulated market.

Learning outcomes: L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S8 | Entrepreneurial Leadership Drivers for design-led growth strategies and how to organise for emergent leadership paths in entrepreneurial ventures. Two organisational philosophies contrasted. In-class strategic simulation provides lived experience of leadership choices under uncertainty, with facilitator-scaffolded debrief on the choices observed and their consequences across the simulated market.

learning_activities.topic:
S9 | Entrepreneurial Analyses

Tools to interpret key attributes of proposed or existing firms, to leverage underlying value and to frame strategic capability-growth actions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: S9 | Entrepreneurial Analyses Tools to interpret key attributes of proposed or existing firms, to leverage underlying value and to frame strategic capability-growth actions.

learning_activities.topic:
S10 | Disruptive innovation

How technological & business model innovations can exploit asymmetric forces, for startups to displace resource-rich incumbents, or yield incremental improvements in product or service deliveries. Examine how innovations disrupt incumbents by crafting new market dynamics for 'good enough' products & services that substitute or complement current offerings

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: S10 | Disruptive innovation How technological & business model innovations can exploit asymmetric forces, for startups to displace resource-rich incumbents, or yield incremental improvements in product or service deliveries. Examine how innovations disrupt incumbents by crafting new market dynamics for 'good enough' products & services that substitute or complement current offerings

learning_activities.topic:
S11 | Entrepreneurial Hypergrowth

Exploring hypergrowth using chasm theory as a mechanism to predict technological dispersion and to pre-empt destructive growth forces. Stages traced from bowling alley through tornado to main street, with attention to the predictable pathologies that derail ventures at each transition. In-class growth simulation surfaces those pathologies as lived decisions, with facilitator-scaffolded debrief on the mistakes encountered.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S11 | Entrepreneurial Hypergrowth Exploring hypergrowth using chasm theory as a mechanism to predict technological dispersion and to pre-empt destructive growth forces. Stages traced from bowling alley through tornado to main street, with attention to the predictable pathologies that derail ventures at each transition. In-class growth simulation surfaces those pathologies as lived decisions, with facilitator-scaffolded debrief on the mistakes encountered.

learning_activities.topic:
S12 | Entrepreneurial Finance

Exploring NPV and reverse financials as tools to interpret growth assumptions and accommodate uncertainty in finance models. Apply finance models to prospective ventures to interpret capital needs and long-term value potential including returns to founders and investors. An in-class ownership-allocation role play closes the session as a final integrative experience — translating finance theory into the lived decision of how value is shared among those who built it.

Learning outcomes: L01, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: S12 | Entrepreneurial Finance Exploring NPV and reverse financials as tools to interpret growth assumptions and accommodate uncertainty in finance models. Apply finance models to prospective ventures to interpret capital needs and long-term value potential including returns to founders and investors. An in-class ownership-allocation role play closes the session as a final integrative experience — translating finance theory into the lived decision of how value is shared among those who built it.

learning_activities.topic:
S13 | Navigating Uncertainty

Reflecting on the journey through the course in preparation for the A3 final exam. The session workshops the live-case research approach and A4 note craft you will use under invigilated conditions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: S13 | Navigating Uncertainty Reflecting on the journey through the course in preparation for the A3 final exam. The session workshops the live-case research approach and A4 note craft you will use under invigilated conditions.
